medical_history.history_of_present_illness: This patient presented with deviation of the trachea to the right significantly. Needle biopsy of substernal mass was consistent with a benign goiter. The connection between the left lobe and the goiter was unclear on CT scan.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Migraines; Substernal goiter, left; Previous surgical history includes removal of substernal goiter, cervical approach, and tubal ligation.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS: 97.8, HR 90, BP 151/95, RR 20, 99% RA. General: well appearing, NAD, clear phonation.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: HEENT: neck incision C/D/I, no neck fullness, no hematoma. Cardiac: regular rate rhythm, S1 S2 normal. Pulmonary: clear, no stridor. Abdomen: soft, nontender, nondistended, normoactive bowel sounds. Extremities: WWP, no CCE.
